title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, TTLs, and Invalidation · Language Lens: Compression Vary
summary: A cache key includes every viewer header, authorization is omitted, the origin receives different data than the cache varies on, and releases rely on global invalidation as routine deployment. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the compression vary increment in cache-key, forwarding, and freshness contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.
Prefer content-versioned names for routine immutable releases and reserve path or cache-tag invalidation for bounded urgent purge with completion evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Compression Vary · Theory Compression Vary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 7450 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to align cache identity, origin forwarding, TTL, versioned names, and urgent invalidation; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
